messages.content:
rewrite this to be pithier and more professional. Also rewrite so that the it's not 'want to check with you and Becky first' but rewrite from perspective of 'we will go with Todd's request unless you feel otherwise': Hi Aaron and Becky,
Today's first readout went well, from AGI’s perspective. By the end of the meeting, Todd and Mark stated the desire to regroup – with the same attendees – for the next session on Monday 6/19. That means, instead of Just the steering committee, the next session will include the steering committee and the design team. I assume that that will be OK given that this ask came from Todd, but i did want to check with you and Becky first. Further the next meeting will be to address some open items requested by Todd during today's meeting and to effectively sign off on design priorities.

Dear Aaron and Becky,
The initial feedback on today's meeting was positive from AGI's point of view. Todd and Mark expressed their interest in convening the same participants on Monday 6/19, expanding the attendees to include the design team. We will follow through on Todd's request, unless you have any objections. This session will tackle open items from today's meeting and finalize design priorities.

rewrite to be pithy and clear:Hi Beckey and Erin,

Today’s readout went well, from AGI’s perspective. Todd and Marc were very engaged. By the end of the meeting, Todd and Marc expressed a desire to convene the same attendees for the next call on Monday 6/19 -- so now that meeting will be a mix of both SteerCo and Design Team. We plan to follow through on their request, unless you have any objections. FYI, given that there was significant alignment from Todd and Marc today, the 6/19 session will focus on addressing open items from today’s meeting and finalizing design priorities.

Hey Becky and Erin,

Positive first readout from AGI today. Todd and Marc expressed interest in involving both SteerCo and Design Team for Monday 6/19 call. We will proceed with their request, pending your objections. Note that next week's session will address open items and finalize design priorities, given Todd and Marc's alignment.

rewrite to be pithy and professional: Hi All,

Thank you for your time and for the productive discussion today. Attached is a post-meeting version of the deck we discussed. Please review and let us know if you have additional thoughts or questions.

Per in-meeting decision, we will regroup for a continuation of Assessment on Monday 6/19 at 10:00AM EST. For that meeting, we will address open items from today's meeting and finalize design priorities. Look forward to the next session!

Best,
Calvin + AGI Team

Hello everyone,

Thank you for your time and constructive discussion today. Please find attached the post-meeting version of the deck we covered. Kindly review and share any additional thoughts or queries.

As per our decision in the meeting, we will have a follow-up session on Monday 6/19 at 10:00 AM EST to continue with the Assessment. The meeting will focus on addressing open items from today's meeting and solidifying design priorities. We are eager to move forward to the next session.

Best regards,
Calvin + AGI Team

rewrite to be clear: Per decision today, we will reconvene the attendees on Monday 6/19 at 10:00 AM EST to continue with the Assessment. The meeting will focus on addressing open items from today’s meeting and solidifying design priorities. We are eager to move forward to the next session.

As decided in the meeting, we will resume the Assessment on Monday 6/19 at 10:00 AM EST with all the participants. The session will center on resolving open items from today's meeting and finalizing design priorities. We look forward to the next meeting.
rewrite to be pithy and professional:

The next Assessment will take place on Monday 6/19 at 10:00 AM EST, per the meeting decision. The objective is to address open items and finalize design priorities. We anticipate the upcoming session.

rewrite to be pithy and professional: Andy, thanks again for your time last Friday and for your participation on today's call. Wanted to follow up on a few items:
Revenue Growth Benchmarking (Excel workbook) - all set here. Thanks for compiling that data.
Missing Plan Calculators - appreciate your continued help tracking down plan calculators for: 'Alternative Sales,' 'RPS,' and 'Transition.'
FY22 Rev / GM / Cost for 10x Job Families - given Todd's interest today in this analysis, would appreciate any update you may have on the data collection here.
Thanks for attention.

Hi Andy,

Thank you for your input on Friday and participation in today's call. Wanted to touch base on a few matters:

- Revenue Growth Benchmarking: The data is compiled. Thanks for your help.
- Missing Plan Calculators: Still searching for 'Alternative Sales,' 'RPS,' and 'Transition.' Thank you for remaining involved.
- FY22 Rev / GM / Cost for 10x Job Families: Todd showed interest in this today. Any updates on the data gathering?

Thank you for your attention.

just started a project with the following premise: Excelitas is evaluating an investment in Heraeus Noblelight – a $200m global provider of high-quality and special purpose photonics and modules in the ultraviolet to infrared wavelength range for industrial, scientific and medical applications.

I have embarked on a project to evaluate Excelitas' investment in Heraeus Noblelight - a global supplier of specialized photonics and modules in the ultraviolet to infrared wavelength range. With a worth of $200m, the company operates across industrial, scientific, and medical domains, offering quality solutions to its clients.

just started a project with the following premise: Excelitas is evaluating an investment in Heraeus Noblelight – a $200m global provider of high-quality and special purpose photonics and modules in the ultraviolet to infrared wavelength range for industrial, scientific and medical applications. Give me 5x fast fact key bullets on both Excelitas and Heraeus Noblelight.

Excelitas:

- Global technology leader in photonics and high-performance sensing with over 90 years of experience.
- Provides innovative solutions to aerospace, defense, healthcare, and other industries.
- Employs over 6,000 people across its 20+ facilities worldwide.
- Offers a broad array of products, including optical sensors, laser modules, detectors, and electronic power supplies.
- Is committed to principles of sustainability, diversity and social responsibility.

Heraeus Noblelight:

- Provides high-quality photonics and modules to customers in the industrial, scientific, and medical sectors.
- Operates globally with subsidiaries and partners in Europe, Asia, and the Americas.
- Offers solutions across a wide range of applications, from curing adhesives to germicidal sterilization.
- Has a strong focus on research and development and currently holds over 500 patents.
- Is committed to protecting the environment and promoting sustainable practices.
I understand Heraeus Noblelight focuses on after-market. What is meant by 'after market?'

I apologize for the confusion, but I did not mention "after-market" in my previous response about Heraeus Noblelight. However, to answer your question, the "after-market" refers to the secondary market where products and services are sold after they have already been in use by the original customer (i.e., the primary market). In the case of Heraeus Noblelight, they focus on providing specialized photonics and modules to customers in the industrial, scientific, and medical sectors. These customers may use Heraeus Noblelight's products in their own end-products, and if they require additional or replacement products over time, they may purchase them from the after-market.
what does it mean to be a 'long-tail customer'?

A "long-tail customer" refers to a customer who purchases niche or specialized products, which often have a lower demand than more mainstream or popular products. 

For example, in the music industry, a "long-tail customer" might be someone who enjoys listening to a specific genre of music that is not widely popular, and they purchase CDs or digital downloads of those niche artists. 

In a business context, a "long-tail customer" might be a company that purchases specialized equipment or components for their products, but they do not have a high volume of purchases. These customers are often valuable to companies as they can provide steady revenue streams over time, even if the individual purchases are relatively low value compared to high volume customers.
